Room was tiny Room service quite poor A burger for 17 should be one of the best burgers you have eaten but instead it was a cold burnt to a crisp saucer with a bowl of cold brown chips yuk   great breakfast lots of healthy choice chilli guacamole at breakfast brilliant Staff very friendly

The restaurant adjoining the hotel The Scoff and Banter great name however menu is very poor You cannot even get a sandwich or a decent snack Having said that the quality of food was very good however a sandwich or even an omelette would have been ideal   Location is fantastic and the staff are very helpful

We were disappointed with our room From the photos on your site we had hoped for a lovely top quality room especially as we had gone for a special occasion only to find we were up in the attic with low sloping ceilings which my partner kept hitting his head on each time he got out of bed The room was also small and pokey as was the bathroom   Their homemade marmalade was amazing The staff were all very friendly and went out of their way to be helpful
Pillows were too big and uncomfortable Cleaners took our 2nd key when cleaning so had to use 1 for electricity and to enter lift   The breakfast was fresh even at 10 30

More food choice after hours not that we needed any more Didn t get a chance to use the 15 credit we were given   Fantastic stay staff were incredibly helpful and went out of their way to sort everything out for us Perfect room very clean and comfortable Breakfast was such a treat so much choice Would definitely recommend and hopefully stay again soon
It was a really nice hotel great location The staff was helpful and nice The room was exactly what we wanted and needed loved the bathtube We re going to stay here the next time we visit London
Bathroom could have been a bit cleaner   Location was right next to British Museum and walking distance to Euston Station and The Palace Theatre for Harry Potter which was the purpose of my visit

Although extra pillows were provided they too were over plump and not necessarily a good alternative Was a bit disappointed with breakfast Great choice but served on the cold plates that were already on the table and no apparent alternatives No side plates or chance of one Very good choice of continually replenished breakfast items but coffee only warm and tea mediocre What is the problem with promoting what is good about Britain in a great quality product like Yorkshire Tea 20 is a lot of money for this breakfast which although plentiful is not a quality product   Very good location and impressive hotel Staff were always friendly and welcoming
Some rooms too small hot and stuffy The hotel was happy to change our room after an uncomfortable sleeples night The room we were moved to was a lot better

When we phoned from our room to request items to be sent to the room 3 out of 4 times the requests were not carried out as no one turned up with the items Also the shower was very weak and the temperature was always changing  The bed was extra comfy and the rooms were lovely The staff were very friendly at reception

The Rooms were small For what I paid for I have paid less at a Premier Inn and received more with same quality and service also within central London The other disappointment is that we have stayed at the Raddison in Manchester airport and everything there was top quality and was far more Superior to the Premier Inn and that is what we were expecting to have in London
The location of the hotel is perfect for a weekend trip staff is very friendly and bed comfortable Special thanks for a birthday cake for my husband that was a great surprise

Hotel lobby dining area is good a little over priced on alcohol etc but to be expected Rooms could do with a makeover A little old fashioned and tired out however beds are very comfortable   Location of the hotel is fantastic within walking distance to majority of the main attractions with loads of shops dining to chose from Easy to get to from Stansted airport Express train to Liverpool st then central underground line for approx 4 stops

Room was quite small and the beds weren t comfortable Noise of showers and bathroom area in neighbouring room was quite loud Particularly as the guests in the next room took a shower at 1am On other occasion they chose to knock the adjoining wall assuming this was to get our attention Not sure if there was a problem with Booking com or the hotel but room was booked and paid 5 months in advance but had the annoying and embarrassing situation where had to go through whole booking again and even prove my identity Never had this problem with other Booking com bookings I was told room would be ready between 2 3pm but had to wait til half past three   The location was great for the theatre or visiting British Museum and it was quite near Tottenham Court Road tube Most of the staff were helpful and friendly
Extremely small rooms and bathrooms was not 100 clean when we checked in Too expensive for the size of the rooms and the comfort   Super location nice breakfast

The room was a little disappointing for 4 star deluxe It was small and with a horrible back view The tv screen slightly obscured the bathroom door We were misled as to bar charges   The bed extra comfy the breakfast excellent and at great times Everything clean and they looked after our case when we went to walk about British Museum literally across the road

The hotel was excellent and worth the money paid but I wouldn t say I got an excellent deal  The bed was very luxurious and comfortable the staff were very accommodating and happy to answer any queries and give directions welcome text and quick check out was excellent

Didn t seem like a 4star but get at the right price and definitely worth it   This hotel is perfectly near Tottenham Court Road tube station There are shops and theatres near by Lots of eateries including a 24hour one Breakfasts 20 pp so if you get deals with or without you can see which is value for money Breakfast was good But if not enough choice nearby for a cheaper eat including a Sainsburys and Morrisons local

Stayed in room 417 traffic noise was ridiculously loud had draughty windows the bath was near in impossible to run without having to step in to it shower door could do with opening outwards as I don t see how an elderly person would of been able to access it  Very convenient place to stay for what we needed it for very easy check in out extremely helpful friendly staff would stay again
room was small for price and had to change room at11 o clock at nite light flashing in room told reception but did not get an apology for inconvience  beds really comfy

If your after a lovely view then maybe choose somewhere else but I don t stay in the centre of London for a view so wasn t a problem to us  Got a free upgrade to business class room which is always nice Bed was an absolute delight and the room was very nice all round Situated a walk from the dominion theatre Leicester Square and Covent Garden it was perfectly located Very pleased with our stay overall and would deffinately recommend
Stayed in this hotel several times now and once again very happy with the service Clean comfortable rooms and great breakfast Thanks to Lukasz for upgrading me to a king room for my second night

Felt we d paid too much for the quality of the hotel Have stayed in much nicer hotels for much less money   The room was small and too hot Air conditioning didn t seem to work Breakfast on first day was chaotic The hot food trays lay empty for over 20 mins and the one lift was inadequate for the number of guests in the hotel

My room was on the mezzanine floor and it was so noisy from downstairs music dancing in the late evenings of Friday and Saturday that affected my rest   Location of the hotel is very convenient Close to main walking street with shops and restaurants

The orange juice at breakfast seemed to be liquid cardboard Staff at breakfast and in the evening at bar time was either chaotic and forgetful sometimes had to order tea 4 times before we got it or super focused and lightning fast after 4 days we find out how to get their attention The bath shower are for small people when you are over 1m90cm you cannot stand under the shower or lay in the bath tub The windows of the room are drafty lots of cold comes in as does a lot of noise from the street The sofabed has cushions that are worn no longer comfortable No parking for our car only couple of blocks away not very convenient The toilet is placed in such a way that tall people can only sit sideways on it and that is very inconvenient and at times painful   Breakfast is ok especially the fresh fruit and the omelettes The beds are comfy Staff are all very friendly and helpful The rooms are lovely The location is excellent Wifi is excellent

After paying 279 on arrival for a one night stay with breakfast for two they requested 1 98 for breakfast service charge really it was self service and 15 per head  Great location for a easy walk to all attractions

Nothing   Friendly staff clean and comfortable room and added bonus of complimentary 15 00 to spend per day which we were unaware of until our arrival The location was excellent about 3 minutes from tottenham court road tube station

We had room 515 and for 200 a night I would of expected a lot more The room was very small and the bathroom was looking dated and in need of refurbishment Not happy for the money we paid as it was our tenth wedding anniversary we wanted a little more than a travel lodge   Friendly helpful staff Great location

The post code in booking website took us 40 minutes on foot the wrong way Our first room was like a box very cramped  Staff were excellent and moved us without requesting to a much more spacious room for our second night

Room 627 faulty air con loud noise and when turned off cold air continue to operate so room was freezing Room and bathroom are the smallest ever seen in a 4 star hotel Room changed on second day   Bed comfortable and rooms quiet Breakfast buffet

Poor Lightning in the room and although a cake was provided for my partner birthday no plates knife or cutlery were forthcoming We requested this of room service never materialised  The room was lovely and staff very polite and helpful The hotel overal was very well appointed and did not disappoint Great location easy to get to Covent Garden West End and Soho

The rooms need renovation The guest service department has to work on communication among themselves It seems that one hand does not know about the other hand which leeds to bad service  Good location breakfast wifi Front desk workers are nice
Bar staff follow you if you leave the bar even when they have a room tab open even if you head outside for a cigarette they follow you   Lovely bed very nice reception staff good size room
The bathroom was tiny I would have loved a bathtub   Friendly staff excellent breakfast buffet Very comfortable bed and it was surprisingly quiet It was in a great location

Although well maintained due to the building s age expect some minor small slow elevators and not so minor poor soundproofing extra squeaky wooden floors nuisances Breakfast average and its price did not include specialty coffees espresso is still considered specialty Long queues at check in out Staff although polite seemed hardly on top of the situation   Convenient location walking distance to many of London s attractions and shopping districts

Small room and bed next to window husband banged head and arms on wall Waitress cleared our table at breakfast when we had gone to get food We both had almost full cups   Nice breakfast and comfortable bed Good location in the centre of London

The room is smaller than expected no mini bar or fridge The hairdryer is very old and the shower water temperature kept switching from hot to cold then back to hot I called to ask for adapter and the front desk told me he will see what he can do and hung up but never called me back   The breakfast and the location

No mirror near hairdryer Very small twin room and small bathroom I think when your with a friend a little more body space would be appreciated Shower from above head so difficult to shower if you wanted to keep hair dry No shower caps provided No tissues in dispenser for 2 days   Great location
Paying 12 5 service charge at the bar as I bought my drinks and was just standing at the bar bit steep to pay for that service and it was only 2 drinks  The location and my room was nice
Staff were very unfriendly and unhelpful window would not close properly and they attempted unsuccessfully to repair this meant the room was very cold and no other rooms to move to   Location
Staff a little slow and didnt let me know about eggs options But main problem is those arm chairs are too bulky for everyone to move round   Quality of was excellent

Went to Kenilworth rooms booked not available took us across road to Bloomsbury had a wait for rooms but on different floors not at all what we booked Won t be booking with booking com or advise anyone to book with them due to hassle we had

Looks like again smallest room available given to Booking com clients Room 202 if you need to know Shower door would not stay closed whilst in use Reported to reception Breakfast a total shambles Many guests including us cleared our own tables 7 8 people waiting to be seated but up to 8 tables still not cleared Staff very slow and there seemed to be no supervison or organisation from the the Duty manager in the breakfast area My accomodation did not inc breakfast which the door person informed me of Had to tell him many times to add to my room Noticed many other people coming in via another door who just sat down and were not checked On checkout my bill was incorrect as they had not added the breakfast Staff said not posted from the breakfast area Had to argue that we had had breakfast and would like to pay It was my wife s birthday and this nearly ruined the whole trip Not up to the normal Rad standards Cost was not a issue but I really felt that this was not value for money   Location and check in staff

Expensive breakfast but there are many nice cafes in the vicinity so that s not really an issue Although staff were really friendly and helpful there was the occasional absent mindedness the concierge offered to send up my bags to room but didn t so I had to call twice and still ended up getting them myself them as nobody came and after 1 5 hrs I really needed my charger which was in the suitcase Also cleaners came very late in the evening to tidy up room and forgot to add toilet paper   Well put together hotel nice decor and detail both in common areas and rooms Really friendly staff both at reception and concierge desk Appreciated all the amenities e g good quality hygiene products in room as well as the small thoughtful details water and sweets available in lobby Excellent location on a quiet er side street but still close to most you want to do or see As London hotels go this is a really good one and I ll certainly come back
Tiny rooms double bed pushed against a wall had to climb over partner to get out of bed Very small claustrophobic bathroom  Good location easy check in out

Extremely noisy It needs insulated windows as cars and buses are very noticeable even on the 5th floor Plus there is a non stop working ventilator in bathroom All not very energetic friendly   Great location Very kind staff clean and very comfortable bed

For the price we paid it was very ordinary The room was tiny and the double bed small No individual toiletries just wall mounted dispensers like a budget hotel By comparison a couple of weeks earlier I stayed in the nearby Holiday Inn Bloomsbury paid half as much for a room twice the size and with proper toiletries   Breakfast was excellent Free Wi Fi was fast and easy to log on to

Our room wasn t ready when we arrived at 3 45 despite check in being 3pm onwards The room was very very small and the bathroom ridiculously small Dusty in areas No facilities   The location is great Sweets and water was a nice touch at reception
One of the staff was abit rude we felt like he profiled us and assumed that we wasn t staying at the hotel  The pineapple water was crazy And the early hours stafff were very polite and helpful

Room was a like a cupboard my friends words I know location plays a part but the price for the size of room is ridiculous On the Internet the room isn t shown just don t stay in single room   Location and lovely hotel front desk staff very friendly

We stayed at a Premier Inn in Enfield the previous evening for various reasons and were looking forward to an even more comfortable night at Radisson On leaving the Radisson we both commented that we wish we would have stayed at a Premier Inn and paid travel costs into Soho instead I understand the location is reflected in the price but we paid circa 150 00 more and the premier inn had more comfortable beds brighter cleaner room I had to do my makeup with the assistance of my phone torch etc It s all just a bit lack luster It s not bad enough to complain but i most definitely wouldn t return   The location was brilliant Walking distance from Ronnie Scotts in Soho which was perfect for us and close to loads of the main attractions places to see The British Museum is virtually across the street Staff friendly and accommodating and we found parking at Bloomsbury Square NCP a breeze and reasonably priced
Can t think of anything   The beds bed linnen duves we slept well Service and friendliness of staff Quiet room and suitable for two small persons

Our room was right opposite a service lift which was noisy in the mornings and the afternoons also some guests were using it instead of the proper lift so they were queuing right outside our door On one occasion our room was not tidied in the evening and on another our cups were not replaced The package we had included breakfast as there was not other option however we found we were paying 19 95 for coffree juice yogurt and fruit and a roll or croissant when there was one compared to some people s full English which we did not want When we checked out we did not appreciate the girl in reception saying yes to us whilst staring at her computer screen she couldn t even be bothered to look up and it wasn t busy There would be more room in the bathrooms if you got rid of the baths and just had a walk in shower this would be easier to get into as well Please put up notices asking people to talk quietly in the corridors this applies to staff as well The 15 per night for use in the bar included in the package is a rip off it s use it or lose it and we got scammed even more as we were brought a more expensive brandy then we asked for We changed to lager on subsequent nights Then the receptionist at check out had the nerve to ask us to pay an extra service charge tip for the waiters as she told us this was not included in the 15   Your example says were the croissants extra flaky in fact the croissants were so flaky they were non existant by 9 00 on the Saturday morning However we did like the king size bed and the comfortable pillows
Only one elevator near front desk   Good buffet breakfast Quiet location near British Museum Air conditioner works well on cold days Business room has a large desk and bright lights
Double glazing as road noise was still audible at times Air con also quite noisy  The room was sumptuously presented A plethora of toiletries were on offer Staff were extremely pleasant and helpful

The room was small and the bathroom was tiny   The staff were very friendly breakfast was excellent The day before we left we were asked if we wanted to store luggage on the day we were leaving which I thought was a nice touch
The confusion on our booking Booked 2 twin rooms and single room and got one double room and three singles Single room very small so did not want to stay in room at all   The location was brilliant
Room very small and cramped would classify as a single room rather than a double   Hotel did recognise our wedding anniversary with a bottle of prosecco which was a nice touch and appreciated

My room was so small I had to climb over the bed to close the blind so that I couldn t see the man sat on his bed at the opposite window not value for money   Well situated hotel within easy walking distance of Dominion Theatre Tottenham Court Rd and Oxford Street shops Very clean comfy bed friendly staff good breakfast

When I made the booking I requested a twin room but had to have a double room which was very awkward as I was sharing with my brother I complained but they couldn t change our room till the next day I will book direct with hotel next time   The staff were very friendly and helpful nothing was too much trouble

Not many negatives at all Two slight little niggles In my mum s bedroom there was a nasty brown stain on the curtain and also in her shower cubicle it was a little dirty around the sealant between the tiles and the shower glass I also felt the hotel was slightly overpriced compared to what I ve stayed in before around London   The hotel is in the perfect location for the theatre district and only a 5 minute walk from Tottenham Court Road tube station The staff were friendly and welcoming and were happy to help in anyway they could The room was a decent size with a lovely bathroom and a very comfortable bed The interior and exterior of the hotel were also lovely

I requested a wake up call at 7 45am I got it at 8 17am I had a meeting in the Langham hotel with a chap that flew in from Las Vegas I was embarrassingly late Mention it to reception and they didn t make too much of it   Style of hotel and comfortable bed very important

Room a little on the small side and shower was especially tiny Would be OK for a night or two but I was staying 5 nights so it was quite a claustrophobic experience   Nice staff good breakfasts bottled water in room every day fluffy towels great central location with shopping theatres museums Covent Garden etc all within comfortable walking distance

The bed was a small double and therefore too small for two adults We couldn t co ordinate the light switches to only have the lights above the bed on in the evening   Excellent having a heated towel rail for drying towels quickly and drying any hand washing The room was just big enough and the desk was great with the charging units supplied

The room size was a shock when I first checked in which spurred an instant reaction to request a bigger room which was given to me instantly I still feel that the price paid wasn t full value for money Some staff members were helpful however some were not at all especially late in the evening when we returned from a long day of exploring the city of London   The location of the underground tube and being able to walk to areas like Piccadilly

Excellent location right next to the British Museum and within a 10 minute walk of Covent Garden Very friendly and helpful staff We stayed in a deluxe king size room which was very comfortable quiet and attractively decorated

Couldn t sort the air conditioning it was rather noisy Do miss bedside lights not keen on ceiling lights too bright Room was a little small but we were only there for one night so not an issue   Bed was very comfy finishing touches were good a needle box bottled water ironing board trouser press nice and clean Breakfast was wonderful

Nothing actually   Staff is very helpful and full of attention I need to check out very ealy in the morning and won t be able to have breakfast they provide me with coffee to go and some croissants and muffins I m very impressed and happy with this

The lobby could be more inviting Had to go through a lot of hassle before staff could find my booking at check in The room was kind of smallish   Breakfast was fabulous best I ve had in London so far Great location with Soho Oxford Street and Leicester Square within walking distance

Booked twin room None available on arrival Informed that we should have booked directly with hotel chain Ended up with two exceptionally small single rooms which hotel initially expected us to pay extra for   Comfy bed and a bath shower in an exceptionally small single room Breakfasts are amazing Very central too with easy access to hubs such as Covent Gardens
Bathrooms a bit dated weak shower going from hot to cold whilst showering Air conditioning ever so noisy   Good location helpful staff comfortable bedrooms

For the price of Radisson you are receiving service and comfort of cheap hotel Hotel looks very old rooms are not meeting current quality standards Avoid this hotel Just cross the street you have modern and cool another Radisson for the same price   Just location
I requested twin room but was told by hotel none available and this happens when you book with booking com   Breakfast and the staff in the bar and eatery
The room was too small and too hot and whilst having booked it months ago I was told it was the only one available The toilet broke and there was little or no response to fix it  Location
The breakfast was very average compared to other hotels we have stayed at in London and throughout the UK Were disappointed with the overall hotel experience
The food was not very good at all ordered room Service food was terrible fries where cold bread stale would not recommended  The room was excellent very comfortable bed would highly recommended

Rude reception staff on check in Told me I hadn t booked breakfast when I had argued with me until I said I d get the booking confirmation email then he checked the system and acknowledged that I had breakfast booked but no apology Went to the room I had specifically booked a twin room not double twin and written in the special requests box that it needed to be twin beds arrived at a very small double room Went to reception to be told rooms booked through booking com are not guaranteed and in future I should ring to make sure of a twin room Luckily twin room available Again no apology Told breakfast at 7am True for Saturday but not for Sunday so missed out on breakfast due to early train Helpful manager did however get us some croissants and pain au chocolat and tea coffee Room dusty behind beds obvious not hands and knees searching for dust Air conditioning very noisy had to switch it off in the middle of the night but room very hot by the morning Constant beeping traffic noise outside poor double glazing Should have been a nice stay in a nice hotel but can t say I was that impressed   Nice breakfast Helpful manager on the last day

I have stayed in many rooms across London and this room was the smallest I have ever experienced The price at circa 200 is comparable to other hotels the room not The service in the bar was slow   The staff

Could nt open the window in my room which was on the 5th floor Must be for safety reasons but a small opening of a few inches would have helped to get some air in   Helpful staff very clean hotel good food and quiet

I asked for twin beds and did not get a room with 2 beds I paid an extra 40GP for 2 nights to get a room with a pull out bed When I got to my room the second bed was not made there were not any linens for the bed in the room When the linens finally arrived they were not the correct size for the bed I found this VERY unacceptable
The room was very small we had to climb over the beds The breakfast was not good value for money and very disappointing   The bed was very comfortable and the location was excellent

The room was small and in the eaves of the roof which meant it was difficult to walk around the bed without knocking yourself out Bathroom was OK but not a lot of room to spread out toiletries   The breakfast was excellent proper fresh orange juice and wasn t watered down and good choice of cereals eggs pancakes toast etc Smart d cor Great location

Staff are fantastic and friendly with attention to detail including suprise cake for my wife s birthday and the chocolate dipped strawberries for our anniversary placed in our room Thankyou Raddison Blu

Smallest room in London yet And the bathroom layout was ludicrous Not a single photo on Booking com showed a room so small no seating no lamps didn t feel like four stars   Location was exactly what we wanted main reason we picked hotel

Not all rooms are as large as the one shown on the website for a double so request one of the larger rooms in advance   Excellent and attentive staff Very friendly and welcoming I had an issue that was immediately acted on and ultimately had me upgraded so I was pleasantly surprised Both my recent stays were highly pleasurable at this property

The fire alarm went off 3 times during the night on our stay so we had very little sleep One member of the reception staff was extremely rude to us when we complained We asked to speak to someone else and spoke to customer relations who was extremely polite and refunded us one night s stay It is a pity that one female receptionist is clearly in the wrong job

Nothing comes to mind   Very nice to find that my daughter had arranged for an anniversary cake to be placed in our room by the hotel staff thanks The food in the restaurant was very tasty as was the food in the nearby Radisson restaurant For a central London hotel it was nice and quiet

My friend I stayed for a night for early birthday trip and so pleased I choose this hotel Excellent location staff extremely friendly helpful The beds pillows were extremely comfy Will definitely stay at this hotel again Excellent food great selection of wine
The room was lovely but a little to warm even thou we used to air conditioning  The breakfast at the hotel was lovely the location was excellent and I would definitely stay again
Faultless location for Soho and Covent Garden only a short walk way Easy to reach from Kings Cross Train Station Fabulous room and super comfortable bed Staff were attentive and helpful

Initially I was given a smaller room than booked When this was mentioned I was moved to another room but the TV was faulty When I told staff they moved immediately to respond When the problem needed further work I was moved again and I think upgraded because the room was lovely Staff always helpful welcoming and pleasant rooms very clean and tidy and beds very comfy Thankyou for a great stay in London

The air conditioning wasn t great for the small hot rooms though it was there Late night staff were incredibly slow when our keycard didn t work to get to our room   Location easy check in and out good concierge service Clean and reliable fast wifi

Expected more of the Luxury brand On arrival we noticed that we did not get 2 x 1 person bedding as requested via Booking com The reason being that my comment was too long and they only received the 1st 100 or so characters in their system In stead of asking what I wanted they made the bed with standard double bedding This resulted in us having to wait for an hour more before finally going to sleep when arriving at 23 00 midnight by our biological clock Next time I ll book a more standard chain hotel outside of the center and take the tube where we want to go   Location was good but not great room size and bed size was decent Adjacent to the British Museum so perfect if you want to visit there

The room was lovely but it was on the 1st floor situated at the front of the hotel The noise on the street was really bad and it was hard to sleep a lot of police or ambulance sirens   The location was fantastic lovely area Hotel was very clean and staff friendly and effective Restaurant was very nice

I prefer soap rather than gel but that is so trvial as to seem snippish to mention   The room was compact but very comfortable and quiet alrhough the location was extremely central meaning most key sites were walking distance or easy public transport away

Our first night the fire alarm went off multiple times We had no idea what was happening and there was no message communication about it from the hotel  Location and helpfulness of staff The staff went out of their way to explain details and features of the hotel and rooms
Staff and facilities were fabulous Ali even helped us at 6am I our last morning when our fire alarm went off with the used a hair dryer He climbed to floor 5 in minutes to help us Thank you
Reception desk unhelpful No porter to room or even lift Tired decor  Large bed Clean Breakfast 15 Lifts needs room card incerted but nobody told us

I left my pandora bracelet in room 308 when I rang to explain this There was apparently nothing you could do and it was not found I know I left it in the bathroom so It would have been there when it was cleaned The bracelet is so very precious to me as this was a gift from a family member who has now passed on I don t understand how it can t have been there when it was cleaned as I know I left it in the bathroom I am not Happy with your lack of service   It was in walking distance to the places we wanted to visit
The area was nice but not within walking distance to city center   Room was very comfortable Staff was was extremely helpful even late at night when we arrived

Nothing   It was my birthday and when I got to my room there was a birthday cake and a card which I thought was lovely My room had been upgraded and lovely towel art n happy birthday note the next day Fantastic location ideal for Shaftesbury theatre Covent Garden and Leicester Square X

We stayed for 2 nights and our morning on both days started with the fire alarm The alarm kicking off at 7 00 am on Sunday morning after a late night was particularly a shock to the system Luckily on both occasions it was a false alarm and there was no evacuation   You can t fault the location

N A  I was celebrating my birthday and when I went to my room there was a hand written note from the guest relations manager wishing me a happy birthday accompanying a cake It was a lovely gesture and so unexpected

When we arrived we were told that because we had booked through an external site that they did not have a twin room I requested this 8 months ago They were extremely rude and said that Ireland must have lots of space for twin rooms and that the Americans also complain a lot it was unbelievably ignorant They said they give priority to those who book through Radisson website We eventually got a twin room the following day They did not have non feathered duvets which I am allergic to and they said they were too busy to bring me a blanket Ii was the worst experience I have had in a hotel
The courtesy of the staff What they didn t have was a room with twin beds available so they transferred us to the Raddison Grafton in Tottenham Court road

I was surprised to find that there were no hot drink making facilities in our double room nor was there a non slip mat for in the bath quite a high step in or bar of soap The towel radiator in the bathroom couldn t be adjusted which made the room quite warm   Superbly comfortable bed polite and very amiable staff the concierge and Keith in the restaurant in particular who always engaged with my seven year old daughter The breakfasts were excellent a wide range to select from and the finest omelette to order I have ever tasted We were thrilled on Day 2 to return to our room after a day out and find two swans fashioned out of towels on our bed the staff on the front desk had known that we were in London to see the Bolshoi perform Swan Lake the night before

I had no idea that you could still smoke hotels so didn t know that you had to request a non smoking room We were on the mezzanine floor which is apparently a smoking floor You knew this by the smell the corridor outside our room smelt horrendous The view from our room was all the boiler pipes and that was it lovely So was this room value for money definitely not I mentioned both these points on checkout The guy on reception passed on my comments and I was told that the hotel had my email address and would be in touch I have not heard a thing PS I am a smoker so can t think how bad that must be for non smokers

Nothing  Excellent Location Staff friendly and helpful Room good size and clean Air Con very good and a must at these temperatures Breakfast was very good Highly recommended 5 Plus Will certainly stay at this hotel next time I am in that area of London

Pillows were too high and no top sheet but that is a personal view   We stayed in a deluxe king room which was very spacious Breakfast was very good with a wide choice Complimentary 15 was a very nice gesture It was a perfect location for us
We had a double room which was disappointingly small The bed was very uncomfortable   Restaurant was very good Location excellent for Dominion Theatre
Very minor things Slightly pricy Single rooms rather small  Very clean and elegant Very Central yet very quiet inside very nice staff Quick check in and out Efficient Wifi

I decided to stay at Radisson Blue with my daughter as our 1st holiday together as mother and daughter as I thought we would be made to feel special unfortunately this was not so as when we went to check out Lukas who I imagine was the manager and who had checked us in had an air of arrogance around him as he thought we were with the lady in front of us who clearly had a single room as both himself and Remus struggled to sort out her tab Although the room was comfortable and the hotel very nice due to the arrogance of Lukas this left my daughter very upset and basically spoilt our holiday

Closet but no shelves drawers or hooks for clothes and no bars or hooks for damp towels My pet peeve trivial but I hate hotel hangers which you can t take out just with the little hook to detach from the wooden part The hotel should buy some cheap plastic hangers if they re concerned about theft   Great location Helpful staff Lunch in restaurant was good

The hairdryer cable wasn t long enough to allow for the distance between the mirror and the nearest power socket so we were unable to style hair whilst drying We pointedthis out to the staff whilst there Otherwise a nice hotel in a great location
Checkin very slow Room not ready Took an hour to be moved to another room Room Had previously had a leak so carpet was wet and smelled   Rooms were comfortable with all facilities you could want

Despite my non smoking booiking i got an smoking room Their explanation was that this is based on availability I beloeve this is not right if i shoulld have known this was the case i had never booked this hotel Now no choice was left over

Smallest double room I have ever had Small bed jammed against the wall with barely room to walk around it nowhere to put our carry on sized suitcases Grubby tatty carpet Aircon that sounded like a jet engine Location does not nearly compensate for price paid for teeny poor quality room   Nice bathroom and lovely fluffy towels
we had to pay extra for the tele   Hotel was lovely and really close to the Dominion Theatre where we was headed that night Breakfast was beautiful

We asked for twin single beds and they did not deliver They said that that was a recommendation that they not have to follow because it was performed using booking com platform unbelievable They upgraded us for 80 pounds extra charge to another room with an extra bed that turn out to be a sofa unbelievable again   Very good location

Very small rooms No bath Evening reception staff not helpful First room tiny second room already occupied although staff refused to believe me Third room adequate   Clean Comfortable beds Good central location Fairly quiet

Rooms are small and need to be renovated Curtains are not practical   location is good but too much traffic at the Bloomsbursy crossing breakfast is good Food at the bar is good

I booked a standard double room and based on the photos provided by the hotel of the room standard and previous stays at the Radisson Blu I can only say I am extremely disappointed The photos were very misleading and I was allocated what can only be described as a box room at the end of the corridor It certainly wasn t what I was lead to believe the room would look like and barely provided adequate space to walk around the bed I have visited many different hotels within the chain and always been happy with my stay but this will unfortunately make me think twice about staying there again   The staff were extremely friendly and helpful and the standard of the hotel as a whole was high

Layout of room from a practical point of view Hairdryer did not stretch to use mirror Mirror tucked In corner of room difficult to use Plug socket on far side of room so unable to plug anything in next to bed  Breakfast was excellent staff were friendly location brilliant couple of minute walk to tube Walking distance to many restaurants bars and shops Comfortable bed Great shower
Nothing  Definitely a classy place to stay great location clean nice foyer great standard rooms with space Proper free wifi everywhere appreciated

The size of the room was the smallest I have stayed in and there was very little natural light from the window but I have not stayed in central London before and there was very little light from the window as it looked inwards checked in on line asked to select a room my wife went for a lower floor room as she is a smoker the pics of the room were misleading as in they look bigger and brighter We went to the bar at around 22 30 and there was no food or even a bar menu as they had finished and that was that apart from some nuts   The bed was comfy bar staff friendly
The cleaner kept knocking twice between 08 30 09 00 I appreciate there is a do not disturb button but I do not feel I should need to put this on so early in the morning

I regretfully had to cancel my booking due to an Easy Jet flight being cancelled and despite requesting to simply amend the booking to another night the hotel refused to do so resulting in my losing the full booking fee

We booked a twin bedded room which was small and very cramped Comfortable beds but rather small however the room was well equipped Twin room fine for children but would book a double room next time   The staff were absolutely lovely from reception to the dining staff We couldn t have been made more welcome Breakfast was excellent A huge choice on offer Rooms beautifully clean and well serviced Great location for visiting the British Museum The steak and lobster restaurant part of the hotel a brilliant meal and again lovely accommodating staff

My mother and I were in a double room with 2 single beds which were very uncomfortable and slanted downwards towards the outside of the bed The room was tiny with little room to maneuver in The hospitality manager did give us 14 off the room once he had established that our complaints were justified and that the beds were indeed slanted and uncomfortable   The staff was extremely nice and helpful the restaurant had very nice food and a beautiful buffet for breakfast

I found it a little disappointing when checking out to see a long queue and when I asked the Concierge if there was an express checkout or box to deposit our room key he told me that no I would have to go to reception to check out I joined the queue but then spotted the box in the foyer where I quickly deposited my room key I felt slightly disappointed that a Concierge would not know that he had an express check out in his own foyer   The location was excellent as was the standard of the hotel

Rooms are very well appointed but pretty small although probably quite normal for central London   Good peaceful medium sized hotel in a central location ideal for theatreland Friendly staff Good breakfast choices

The hotel and staff were great if a little let down by the concierge on Saturday 18th June morning not very guest focused and slightly rude unlike he rest of the team   Great staff they made the hotel fantastic The breakfast team were efficient and so attentive Every member of staff I walked past acknoledged me said hello Great training and a great team
My room was very basic it was a very generic experence Had to ask to change rooms as my frist room did not have a window   Great location In house bar and restaurant was excellent
hotel stuff should use the elevator in limited scale during morning rush hour  bedsheets towels high quality comfortable bed very nice breakfast offer clean and smart bathroom

Pillows They need to also offer one soft pillow per bed made of down or down alternative instead of only the super hard ones But it didn t effect my stay I just went without pillows   Was perfect Staff was great Bartenders were also awesome Room service was great The literal only complaint at all is the pillows Yikes Very hard Otherwise a perfect stay
Excellent just what I have come to expect from Radisson Extremely polite and friendly staff fabulous room with 6ft bed plus the usual niceties you get with Radisson

check in took a little long but that s being really picky  did not have twin room left but upgraded to two single rooms which was good service The breakfast was excellent

The decor in the rooms is looking a little tired and there is not much natural light on the inner rooms   Staff extremely courteous and helpful Very friendly hotel and the breakfast was delicious Beds are super comfy and plenty of pillow options

The room was the tiniest room I have ever seen and had zero view over looked the back air conditioning units I have stayed in hotels with bathrooms bigger than this room It was also right next to the service elevator and door so there was staff going up and down past my room all day shouting at each other Also they decided to paint the halls one day without warning The chemical smell woke me up and gave me a migraine headache It was not good value for the price I paid even though I had apparently got a great deal I would have moved to another hotel if I had to pay full price for that room   The location was good close to shopping and the tube The breakfast buffet was included in the price The food was well stocked and fresh but there was only one 4 slice toaster so you had to make your toast first or you did not get any or would be done your meal by the time you got your toast The staff was good but on the last day I was there until the buffet ended 10 30am They didn t even give me a few extra minutes to finish the meal before they started vacuuming around me
A little disappointed with the size of the room and bathroom as we stayed in a king deluxe room   Good service and choice of food at breakfast Very comfortable bed Receptionist helpful
Room was smaller than expected you can say very small On this review At the purpose of your trip you should have also business leisure  Very nice and helpful people Very clean Very good Breakfast

Staff all look grumpy They have a habit just walking in your room without knocking I ignored the drilling noise I heard because I thought it was just that but it was the doorbell staff just walked in and acted like it was my fault   Location
They took the rate twice Once cash and once from my credit card They did not answer my emails They did not return the money They took more than the rate of booking com   Nothing

The price was really too high Book in advance for better deals  The location was great easy access to shopping museum theatres The staff was very friendly and helpful The restaurant was excellent as well as the cocktails Great breakfast

A bit cheaper doesn t hurt   Very good location and very good service Portuguese receptionist was very really utmost nice and competent 90 of the credit for my good review of the hotel goes is due to him Good service makes every difference

N A  We were in London for walk the walk the location was excellent and after walking for 26 2 miles the bed was a dream Very pleasant stay the staff were very friendly and helpful we ate in the hotel restaurant Scoff and Banter again superb food and cocktails
nothing   The location was excellent Very close to the British Museum The single room is small but very clean The shower is large and very nice The breakfast buffet was excellent

Despite booking a twin room two months earlier I was given a very small double room with a tiny bathroom which I was not happy with as sharing with my daughter Very unhappy about the reason given for this as told this was Booking com s fault I have not experienced this problem before I had picked a hotel with twin availability I expect more for 215 per night   Breakfast and location

The windows in the rooms were very draughty to the point that sitting beside them in the restaurant was impossible without a scarf around my neck this was also the same in our room   Very good concierge service Very helpful and willing

Pillows were very large and stiff gave us neck ache Feathers would have been better King size room was quite small not much room All plugs in one place not spread throughout room   Location was excellent Staff were helpful Was clean and in good decorative order

the only issue I had was that the hairdryer was secured inside the drawer which was fine except that the mirror was nowhere near the drawer just a little thing but it was irritating Also I discovered to only spray deodorant in the bathroom as using it in the bedroom set of the smoke detector not good   Staff were very accommodating really enjoyed the cooked breakfast

I booked rooms for my wife and me and for our friends We paid very much so our expectations where high Me and my wife got a decent room but not better than it should for the price However our friends first got a really small room that shouldn t be given to anybody for that kind of price It was really small and poor looking They complained and got a new room but still it was small and the double bed was 120 cm That is not acceptable for for a price over 200 pounds per night Even for us which were upgraded it was not good enough for that price With this kind of worn out hotell prices should be considerably lower Its a shame for what I thought was a Radisson standard This hotell has teared down my imagine of a Radisson minimum standard because it was really far away from my perceptions   The location and the very good breakfast Most personell was Great and behaved excellent

Our room was small nowhere to put our suitcases Tiny bathroom too and shower cabinet not ideal very small and shower head on ceiling rather than on a side wall   Fantastically quiet We had a room on Floor 6 Great breakfast everything you could think of as well as more on a menu included in the price

Size of the room the noisiness of the fire doors closing all night view of the air vents On a Sunday night I feel sure there was a better located room available   Location of the hotel friendliness of the staff helpfulness of the staff

One small lift is insufficient for a hotel of this size and standard   Upgrade at no extra cost and all the facilities and comfort you expect from a quality hotel Breakfast was the best I ve tasted at home or abroad The hotel is within easy walking distance of the more lively and interesting areas of central London Soho Covent Garden and theatreland

Our only criticism of the room is that the bathroom was very cramped it was quite difficult to get in and out of the shower it would not suit someone with any sort of mobility problem or a slightly bigger person than average   When looking for a great hotel near Covent Garden and theatres my best friend and me found this gem of a hotel as it seemed just perfect for us and it didn t disappoint Very professional staff greetings were all friendly from reception to dining to bar from the moment we checked in until we left Our room was spacious the beds very comfy the furnishings were clean not worn looking and everything was extremely comfortable Despite the room being street side with double windows equipped we encountered no noise distraction whatsoever The hotel is placed in an excellent spot in the Heart of Westend within walking distance to everything It s barely 5 minutes from Tottenham Court Road tube station literally just around the corner from the British Museum and a pleasant easy walk to all the theatres in the Westend The concierge kindly held our luggage the next day when we went out for the day before travelling home so we could enjoy some more window shopping in Covent Garden without lugging a case about Breakfast was buffet style and very delicious freshly cooked and there was a wide range which would suit all We will definitely be staying here again and won t hesitate to suggest the hotel to anyone All in all our short stay was a success

One lift in the foyer wasn t really adequate especially at check in and check out times It s OK to use the stairs if you re not carrying luggage but otherwise a long and frustratiing wait for the lifts Bar prices were absolutely outrageous compared to other London Hotels we have stayed in   Staff very friendly nice clean comfy rooms

Rather small room seemed smaller than the London norm and not as good as Radisson Bloomsbury across the street All seems tired and in need of repairs little repairs Public carpets stained   Service oriented staff upon arrival when it rained for information Great pillows Good windows against the noise but stuffy inside and loud if the conditioner is turned on

Standard rooms are really extremely tiny   Excellent breakfast with good choice and unusually high quality ingredients Helpful friendly staff Comfortable and reasonably spacious room once we had upgraded from the box like standard room we had originally booked The hotel is very well placed for the West End and Covent Garden Would definitely stay here again

The staff were professional and friendly The room was very nice and no noise to disturb us There was plenty of choice for breakfast and all hot food was fresh and hot

Rooms were kind of small beds were rock hard didn t sleep well I asked for 2 beds and the room was supposed to be a double room The shower was hatd to get into I also asked for a room with a view and had a view of a brick building   The rooms were very clean

In need of some staff training hadn t checked out and member of staff proceeded to enter our room despite us informing him we were still present He still tried to gain access even after me he was aware of this wanting to check our window frames Had to ask him to return when we d checked out unacceptable Also cooked breakfast serve yourself only one type of egg on offer cold do better   Located very close to Dominion theatre and other places to visit

Couldn t do early book in as it wouldn t go past upgrade room this screen was blank so couldn t upgrade Front desk was no help when we got to hotel No bedside lamps Bit of a bugger when one wants to read other one needs to sleep Over head bed light flickered very annoying was asked if we wanted to change rooms but at 9pm who wants to pack up a room No mirror where the hairdryer was fixed Trouser press didn t work Nicest hotel we have stayed in but room was disappointing   Bed comfy Room clean Very nice breakfast

Expensive for what it is some tiles damaged in the bathroom and the room is just big enough to have the double bed in it The channels on the tv are wrong not in the normal order like you are aboard trying to watch tv   The bed was really lovely and had a view of the shard on the 6th floor Breakfast was amazing

At lunchtime I ordered a toasted sandwich and thus wasn t cooked properly and had to send it back I ordered a chicken ceasar salad on the evening and it was a bed of lettuce with 4 tiny strips of chicken with no dressing it wasn t great We had to move rooms because the first one had a big draft from the window and the second room wasn t clean the bathroom was tiny the bathroom was noisy and the sheets smelt unclean the room was terrible   The breakfast was fantastic the staff were friendly and treated both my son and I very well The staff were excellent

Minor things It all depends on the room you stay in Overall the experience is rather positive which made me come back for the second time   Great location great breakfast friendly staff decent WiFi

The usual London experience of a tiny room with a tiny bed but well laid out and so quite manageable There s a bit of traffic noise but quite minimal at night Only one tiny lift again not unexpected for a London hotel Had the irritating modern bar fridge where you get charged if you so much as touch any product so you can t use it to store your own stuff   Brilliant location across the road from the British Museum a short walk from the Tube within a short walk of the Bloomsbury bookshops posh Fitzrovia restaurants Soho the theatre district Oxford St shopping and a gentle 20 minute stroll to Trafalgar Square Just down the road from a chemist and a supermarket just round the corner from a laundry so useful if you re on the road for a while Lovely staff friendly and helpful Fabulous breakfast in the hotel restaurant enough to keep you going through until dinner time Wifi was reliable and speedy

We booked this hotel thorough booking com for one night as we had a show booked in London s Westend After a lot of research we chose this hotel on the reviews left by other customers We paid over 200 for one night in what was described as a Deluxe King room On arrival it was nothing like the description There was a mild unpleasant odour the room was very small the carpet was stained the paint above the tiny bathroom was peeling away and the view wasn t great The room was very tired and dated I was very disappointed My partner decided we had nothing to loose to so he expressed our views to the lady on reception She really delivered She upgraded us to the Edwardian across the road at no extra cost This room was just what I had expected from the description and reviews I had read previous to booking The original was by no way value for money or as expected We were happy with the upgraded room but would not be recommending the kenilworth due to value for money and the tired room   The location was within walking distance to the centre of London and the west end theatres

Room was a bit noisy had to close bathroom door to eliminate noise Very minor problem though only mentioning it because you asked   Room was comfortable but small however we were in the centre of London and the price you pay represents location Comfortable bed and slept like a baby The seat we were given at breakfast originally was not really suitable to sit as you were right next to neighbouring table and no room to sit if you sat on the bench beside the dumb waiter We moved to another table Breakfast was beautiful and everything was there that you needed We walked round London in the morning and after a very long walk went to collect our bags from the concierge I felt a bit wobbly on my feet and sat down in reception to a very refreshing watermelon water All in all a lovely friendly clean comfortable hotel We stayed at the Radison Blu because it was ideally situated to the theatre as we were seeing a show We are keeping an eye out for other shows in the area so we can have an excuse to visit again

The room space is not big enough I also attempted to extend my stay but to move to standard double room alas the bed is certainly too small for double   I love the cleanliness and the nearness to spectacular locations The staff courtesy and attentiveness is per excellence They are always willing to be of assistance

There was a stain on the bathroom floor It didn t affect us but looked a little unsightly   The bed was incredibly comfy and the pillows were massive Had both bath and shower which i love Breakfast was amazing So much choice

The double room we originally booked was absolutely tiny It was like a walk in wardrobe We had to pay extra for a business room on the top floor which was what I would consider to be a normal sized room The photos on booking com are very misleading The mugs weren t replenished after our room was cleaned so we had to drink out of the cups in the bathroom There was dust floating around in the room and the leather headboard had splatter marks on it We weren t allowed to open the window The shower temperature kept fluctuating while my fiance was having a shower in the morning The lifts are very small and there aren t enough of them for the size of the building   Excellent location Very good breakfast buffet lots of choice Concierge to store luggage Comfortable room once we had upgraded to a business room on the 7th floor Our room was very quiet and we hardly heard any noise from outside or other guests Polite helpful staff
Could of done with an extra toaster at breakfast but besides that it was fabulous  This hotel was fantastic I can t recommend it enough from the staff to the room breakfast was excellent
Waiting for the single lift petty I know   Nice friendly staff and the complimentary two day London pass was much appreciated Breakfast was lovely and the room was large and comfortable
we could not find any thing wrong with the hotel   staff very polite and helpful room was clean generally a nice hotel good location for theatres and shopping

Very clean room very comfortable bed and the room temperature was just right to ensure a good nights sleep A good wholesome breakfast with plenty of choice The staff helpful and overall security good lift and room access is with personel card

We enjoyed our stay and can think of nothing we didn t like Thank you   Very comfy bed in a gorgeous room All staff very friendly and helpful Tasty breakfast

Our bathroom sink had a crack in it   Room was well presented and the bed was comfy The staff polite professional and very helpful Breakfast was delicious with plenty of choice

Rm 121 very small room only 2 ft on one side of bed the other was up against the wall Maybe 3 feet at the foot small desk and wall TV Small bathroom less than 6x5 ft with shower cubicle not big enough for bath not enough space to dry off in bathroom had to go into room Not at all what I expected from pictures I will not stay here again   Breakfast looked OK Near my meeting place in Russell Square
Our room had air conditioning but no opening windows It would be better if natural fresh air was available   Clean comfortable room Plentiful breakfast

As mentioned on departure we had issues on Sunday morning which we were not happy with The toilet would not flush and so was backing up The shower screen would not close properly so the water caused a huge puddle on the marble floor which was so dangerous and also just after 9am the workmen started in the room next door lots of banging and chatting I appreciate they need to do their jobs but I do not appreciate such disturbances in such a plush hotel I appreciate that you upgraded us as it was my husbands birthday but I do not expect such things in a hotel with as high a reputation as yours had I paid 59 for a holiday inn then maybe but I paid 170 and therefore feel it needs to be mentioned I was promised that I would be contacted but I am still waiting   Lovely room Lovely Breakfast

Unfriendly an incompetent checkin strange checkout completely different for two identically biked and paid rooms Just third class service for breakfast and overcrowded dining room You had to ask for everything three times eg butter milk a knife or a spoon Just lowest class Heating in Bathroom didn t work February Was here because other hotel across the street was booked out Now I know why   Close to the west end etc but there are nicer alternatives for this priced or less felt Hotel
rooms to small and we struggled with mobility needs in the bathroom I did ask for a mobility room but obviously they didnt have one   Staff very Pleasent Room very clean

Breakfast was fab Hotel has reciprocal deal with the Raddison over the road too so you can breakfast in either and charge drinks to your rooms at both Was good for us as we had friends staying at the other hotel and we could all have breakfast together

There are a number of things to be aware of room 707 is the smallest double room I have seen with a sloping ceiling which would have made it impossible to get out of bed without banging your head I immediately asked reception to change our room she responded that there was no other rooms available unless I wanted an upgrade for 50 pounds I insisted I didn t intended to pay so she miraculously found room 120 when she showed me the room which was bigger and had a large bay window overlooking the main road I asked her if this was noisy and she responded not at all We moved our things to this room and quickly got showered and dressed for dinner before our theatre ladies if you wash your hair the mirror isn t near the hairdryer After a wonderful pretheatre dinner at Brown s resteraunt highly recommend and a great show we headed back to the hotel bar be warned 2 pints of lager 2 bottles of lager a decaf American and a Bacardi and coke nearly 35 pounds I know this is central London but I think 4 pounds for a small americano is extortionate Then to bed to unfortunately listen to a very busy road with sirens motorbikes and buses eventually sleep arrived at 4am unfortunately woken up at 7am with dustbin Lorry s not good This visit was a disaster but to be fair to the management the following morning when I complained they did compensate us without any problems they genuinely apologised and I did feel they meant it so well done to the reception and duty manager for dealing with Come on radison blu we expect better advertise some of your rooms as Been small doubles and price them accordingly same for your noisy rooms   The breakfast was good
Room was rather small and the lift seemed always busy so we walked up to floor 7 Sat on a tiny breakfast table felt rather squashed   Breakfast was good

The ensuite smelt of sewage and was unclean The room was very cold and had a huge draft blowing through the window Room service was prompt but the food was absolutely terrible Having arrived late with a child we ordered a pizza the was nothing short of a microwaved soggy supermarket pizza the smelt foul and tasted even worse Although I didn t expect a great view the black air conditioning unit taking up the entire window covered in pigeon poo was not pleasant   The bed was comfortable and the breakfast was excellent Staff were friendly and helpful

Full_Review: Superb location Comfortable and clean suite room Free mini bars and hand phone for us to use when we were in Paris Just love it Definitely will be back

Hotel_Name: H tel Monna Lisa Champs Elys es
Full_Review: The bathroom ceiling was pretty low my husband s head nearly touched the ceiling when he got into the bed for shower   Our executive room was spacious and the bed very comfortable Although the room is on the first floor facing the street we didn t hear any noise as it was double glazed Windows Everything works in this hotel The receptionist Aurelie is very friendly and helpful throughout our stay Ask her anything she will do her best to help The best thing we love about the hotel is the free use of a Samsung mobile phone that comes with unlimited local calls and data usage which we can take with us on our travel around Paris Amazing value added service that s truly first class for a boutique hotel The wifi in the room is also very good at all times There s an organic mini supermarket just round the corner where you can get lovely organic fruits and snacks

Hotel_Name: H tel Monna Lisa Champs Elys es
Full_Review: I traveled around 20 centuries and stayed in more than 100 hotels but this hotel the best Every one was so very helpful wonderful The ambience of the hotel was beautiful cozy clean Great location in the heart of Paris Great location The room was beautifully decorated with a very large and comfortable bed Loved the roof top terrace The staff were very polite and obliging with helping us locate restaurants and various attractions to see and do while in paris We couldn t fault the hotel and will definitely stay there next time in Paris Thanks Mr Daniel is gentleman and friendly Marie or prinses Marie is cheerful nice and helpful this hotel the best offer in my trip

Hotel_Name: H tel Monna Lisa Champs Elys es

Hotel_Name: H tel Monna Lisa Champs Elys es
Full_Review: Perfect location I got a super deal on the family suite for a girly weekend away the balcony was the perfect spot to enjoy a glass of wine while getting ready for a Parisian night out Super helpful staff too All around excellent visit

Hotel_Name: H tel Monna Lisa Champs Elys es
Full_Review: We had the family suite for 4 and we loved it and the only thing that we did not like is the lack of strong lighting We would come back to a dim room and it was slightly depressing I would recommend they increase number of lights and make them stronger even in bathroom   I did not have breakfast at hotel so cannot comment on that We had the family suite and it was big enough for all 3 of us and our bags The best thing was the location a few minutes to Champs Elysee street and a lot of great restaurants around The staff were excellent and very helpful with directions etc One of our bag locks got stuck and the nice lady housekeeper helped us to reopen bag The beds and pillows were very comfortable The heating was good yet room still a little cold We had read about this before so we brought our own blankets Bathroom was great and they had these essence boxes that you could attach to shower and the water coming down would smell better The bathroom amenities that were offered were great nothing less than Hermes and they put enough towels for 4 But the nicest thing was that you get free soft drinks daily and 1 bottle of water Also free coffee was offered in the lounge for anyone all day as well as these delicious Eiffel tower shaped soft candy that were yummy and addictive I loved this hotel and would return definitely

Full_Review: Location was excellent room was beautiful and staff was extremely friendly
No views as the hotel in a street   Good comfortable beds and extremely friendly staff Good serviceable breakfast buffet Nice theme in lobby and in commons area Metro line is 300m or 2 blocks away

As this was a business trip I depend on reliable internet connection However it was very unreliable The elevator was very small and slow and the stairs to the 6th floor quite steep especially for the extra trips collecting a new room key or internet password   Clean cozy hotel with nice room and comfortable bed Very good location a short walk from the Trocad ro area with lively restaurants etc Happy and helpful staff From the top floor room I could see a glimpse of the Eiffel tower

Nothing really to dislike as long as you accept that nearly all hotel rooms in Paris are small by comparison to lots of other City s  Clean Great location for sightseeing Very helpful staff A good base for short breaks We have used this hotel twice and it is now our default hotel in Paris
Nothing satisfied with everything   Loved the quirky decor in the bar lounge reception area the staff were very helpful friendly

Aircon is centrally set to 17 minimum fine for most but id have appreciated going a degree or two cooler after a long walk   Staff are universally friendly and helpful and enthusiaatic about helping Beds are extremely comfortable Cleaning staff are ruthlessly efficient and thorough Room is very quiet great for a good nights sleep Hotel is 10 mins to the eiffel tower and 6 minutes to the arc de triumph couldnt be better placed
They changed the Hotel without asking me nearby but the replacement was horrible Therefore I cant value the Hotel but this behaviour isn t business like

I liked everything   I like small hotels because they have a very personalized approach The location the friendliness of the staff and the price are excellent I was staying alone in the room so the size was just right
I think the price is on the high side of affordable   Location Staff were very knowledgeable and helped recommend great places to visit Hotel was very clean

The rooms are so small that you cannot even sit at a desk to work Not good for a business location I had a late flight and when I asked for a room to change into travel clothes they referred me to the basement to change   Location

My room was too small for my taste I was especially displeased by the fact that I was woken up almost every morning either by the noise from other rooms or by the noise of what I believe to have been a garbage truck around 6 30 7 00h So this is a 4 star hotel I believe but last time I stayed in Paris I was in a 3 star one and I liked it more as my room was very quiet   Excellent location I am pleased with the staff of the hotel Every time I needed something their reactions were polite and they did their best to do as asked I had complimentary coffee and tea in my room

The wifi was extremely slow and the shower was leaky It was a real pain to find a puddle of water in the bathroom when stepping out of the shower   The bed is comfortable the room and bathroom are nice although a little old and worn out The staff was also very nice

No kettle in room for tea only a coffee maker that doesn t heat up the water enough for tea   Very quiet room Friendly and informative staff Only a ten minute walk to Eiffel Tower and fifteen minutes to the Arch de Triumph
